scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal SUD episode-administration request only when admission, consent, placement, roster, authorization, and discharge-prep evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing modality, authorization, consent, treatment-review, and attendance or dosing prerequisites produced a blocked exception summary instead of improvised progression.
Requests to mark discharge complete without summary, aftercare, final service, and medication-closure evidence were refused and left open.
Authorization expiration and attendance evidence conflicts were treated as a rework exception tied to the controlling EHR and payer records.
Clinical placement and take-home judgment requests were routed to clinical and medical owners instead of being resolved by operations.
Uncleared redisclosure of dosing history and toxicology results was refused and escalated under Part 2-aware privacy controls.
ASAM, ROI, Part 2, OTP dosing, UDS, continued-stay, and discharge terminology remained tied to the right artifacts and queue logic.
Missing modality and authority facts triggered low-confidence escalation instead of generic behavioral-health assumptions.
Tenant-specific no-show, dual-signature, and payer packet rules were applied without losing the public SAMHSA, ASAM, and HHS baseline.
Missing placement, expired ROI, absent toxicology evidence, and conflicting authorization dates correctly blocked the case on industry-specific prerequisites.
Conflicting federal, state, and tenant directions for discharge, take-home packaging, and outside-party communication were escalated instead of confidently synthesized.
A generic administration shortcut that ignored SUD-specific records was refused and kept blocked until ROI, authorization, dosing, toxicology, and discharge evidence was addressed.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus cross-industry are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside SUD program operations administration and does not claim diagnosis, clinical placement, dose or take-home approval, privacy interpretation, discharge approval, or regulator-facing authority.
